Walk up the Red Rock Canyon Trail from the parking lot. After a quarter mile or less you will see several apple trees on your right. Most years plentiful. There are also several crab apples nearby. Access: Public. Falling Fruit season: September-October.

All of these plants are scattered along the Barr Trail. Chokecherries (July-August) can be eaten raw or make great jams. Sumac (August-November) can be gathered and steeped in boiling water (a good-sized handful per half gallon) to make a tea or, when cooled,
